findings.message: `APP_WIDGET_API_URL: "${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}"` interpolates `${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}` but no `APP_WIDGET_API_URL` is declared in `.env.example` (only APP_ENVIRONMENT, APP_DATABASE_URL, APP_LOG_LEVEL, APP_OTLP_ENDPOINT, APP_SECRET_KEY, APP_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are). With no declaration, compose substitutes an empty string, silently injecting a blank var into the app container. Additionally, `base.yml` is a DEV/TEST-ONLY overlay (it supplies the local build context, `APP_ENVIRONMENT: dev`, and Traefik labels) and is NOT part of the staging/prod deploy composition — so this variable wiring reaches dev/test only and will never reach staging/prod, even though the value (a widget API URL) is plainly an app-runtime concern that prod also needs.
findings.suggestion: Declare `APP_WIDGET_API_URL=` (with a safe local-dev default) in `.env.example` so the interpolation resolves, and wire the variable into the deploy-reaching overlay (the self-contained `staging.yml`/`prod.yml` app service or `services.yml`) rather than only `base.yml`, so staging/prod actually receive it. If the app consumes it, ensure a matching pydantic `APP_`-prefixed field exists in `src/*/config/settings.py`.
